questions.learningObjective: State the primary services provided by a business aircraft connectivity system.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of possible services, identify those provided to passengers and crew by a business aircraft connectivity system.

flashcard.front: What service does a business aircraft connectivity system provide to passengers and crew?
flashcard.back: Internet access and voice communications for cabin users.

jeopardy.clue: The two core cabin offerings from a business jet connectivity system.
choices: Electrical load management
Flight management calculations
Engine trend monitoring feeds
Internet and voice communications

multipleChoice.question: In a corporate-jet cabin, what function is the onboard connectivity suite primarily installed to deliver for those onboard?
choices: Engine trend monitoring data is the primary service offered.
Flight management computations are the primary service offered.
Electrical load management is the primary service offered.
Internet access and voice communications are the primary services.

practice.scenario: An owner is comparing the connectivity package on two Challenger 3500s during a demo. They ask what the connectivity system actually provides to passengers and crew.
practice.question: What should you tell them the system provides?
choices: It provides engine trend monitoring data for maintenance.
It provides flight management computations for the FMS.
It provides electrical load management for the buses.
It provides internet access and voice communications.

preassessment.question: Which service does a cabin connectivity system provide?
choices: Engine trend monitoring feeds are provided.
Flight management computations are provided.
Electrical load management is provided.
Internet and voice communications are provided.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the data protocol used for high-speed CMS device communication.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of data bus options, identify the protocol modern cabin management systems use for high-speed device-to-device transfer.

flashcard.front: Which protocol do modern CMSs use for high-speed data transfer between cabin devices?
flashcard.back: Ethernet-based networking, which provides the bandwidth needed for streaming A/V and control traffic.

jeopardy.clue: This IEEE networking technology gives modern cabins the bandwidth to stream HD video between devices.
choices: Ethernet-based networking
MIL-STD-1553 data bus
ARINC 429 twisted-pair bus
RS-422 serial interface

multipleChoice.question: On contemporary business aircraft, what networking technology enables rapid communication among cabin devices connected to the CMS?
choices: Ethernet-based networking carries high-speed cabin data.
MIL-STD-1553 data bus carries high-speed cabin data.
ARINC 429 twisted pair carries high-speed cabin data.
RS-422 serial interface carries high-speed cabin data.

practice.scenario: During a retrofit design review you must pick the backbone the new CMS will use to move streaming A/V between head-end and seatback displays.
practice.question: Which protocol do modern CMSs use for high-speed device-to-device transfer?
choices: Ethernet-based networking is the CMS high-speed backbone.
MIL-STD-1553 is the CMS high-speed backbone.
ARINC 429 is the CMS high-speed backbone.
RS-422 serial is the CMS high-speed backbone.

preassessment.question: Which protocol carries high-speed data between CMS devices?
choices: Ethernet-based networking carries the high-speed data.
MIL-STD-1553 carries the high-speed data.
ARINC 429 carries the high-speed data.
RS-422 serial carries the high-speed data.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the component that routes data between passenger devices and the SatCom system.
questions.fullObjective: Given a connectivity architecture, identify which component handles routing between passenger devices and the satellite communication system.

flashcard.front: Which component manages data routing between passenger devices and the SatCom system?
flashcard.back: The cabin network router; it sits between cabin Wi-Fi clients and the SDU/modem path to the satellite.

jeopardy.clue: This cabin LRU is the traffic cop between passenger tablets and the satellite modem.
choices: Flight management computer
Cabin network router
Satellite data unit modem
Mode S transponder

multipleChoice.question: During a SatCom-based connectivity session, what hardware element directs traffic between passenger seat devices and the uplink transceiver?
choices: The satellite data unit modem handles cabin device routing.
The cabin network router handles routing to the SatCom path.
The flight management computer handles cabin device routing.
The Mode S transponder handles cabin device routing.

practice.scenario: A passenger cannot reach the internet from an iPad on a Gulfstream G280 even though the SatCom link shows a healthy signal. You are tracing the data path.
practice.question: Which component manages routing between passenger devices and the SatCom system?
choices: The Mode S transponder manages this routing.
The cabin network router manages this routing.
The flight management computer manages this routing.
The SatCom data unit modem manages this routing.

preassessment.question: Which component routes data between passenger devices and SatCom?
choices: The SatCom data unit modem routes the data.
The cabin network router routes the data.
The flight management computer routes the data.
The Mode S transponder routes the data.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the antenna type that tracks satellites electronically without moving parts.
questions.fullObjective: Given antenna options, identify which type tracks a satellite electronically during aircraft maneuvers.

flashcard.front: Which antenna type tracks a satellite electronically during maneuvers?
flashcard.back: An electronically steered phased array; beam direction is controlled by phase, not mechanical motion.

jeopardy.clue: This antenna technology shifts its beam via phase control rather than a gimbal motor.
choices: Mechanically steered parabolic dish
Fixed-mount dipole antenna
Electronically steered phased array
Helical omnidirectional antenna

multipleChoice.question: Because a business jet rolls and banks in flight, the SatCom antenna must keep the beam pointed at the spacecraft. What antenna design performs that pointing without moving parts?
choices: A helical omnidirectional antenna tracks via phase control.
A mechanically steered parabolic dish tracks via phase control.
An electronically steered phased array tracks via phase control.
A fixed-mount dipole antenna tracks via phase control.

practice.scenario: You are specifying a Ka-band antenna for a retrofit on a Global 7500. The owner wants zero moving parts on the radome.
practice.question: Which antenna type tracks the satellite electronically during maneuvers?
choices: A fixed-mount dipole antenna meets the requirement.
A mechanically steered parabolic dish meets the requirement.
An electronically steered phased array meets the requirement.
A helical omnidirectional antenna meets the requirement.

preassessment.question: Which antenna tracks a satellite electronically during maneuvers?
choices: A fixed-mount dipole antenna tracks electronically.
A mechanically steered parabolic dish tracks electronically.
An electronically steered phased array tracks electronically.
A helical omnidirectional antenna tracks electronically.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the body that publishes avionics interface standards enabling multi-vendor interoperability.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of aviation organizations, identify which publishes the interface standards that allow avionics from different manufacturers to communicate.

flashcard.front: Which organization publishes the standards that let avionics from different manufacturers talk to each other?
flashcard.back: The Airlines Electronic Engineering Committee (AEEC), which produces the ARINC specifications.

jeopardy.clue: This AEEC-led body is the reason a Honeywell box can exchange data with a Collins box without drama.
choices: North Atlantic Treaty Organization
International Civil Aviation Organization
Federal Aviation Administration
Airlines Electronic Engineering Committee

multipleChoice.question: For cross-manufacturer avionics interoperability, which industry body is responsible for writing the applicable interface standards?
choices: The International Civil Aviation Organization publishes these standards.
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization publishes these standards.
The Federal Aviation Administration publishes these standards.
The Airlines Electronic Engineering Committee publishes ARINC standards.

practice.scenario: A design engineer needs to reference the specification that defines ARINC 664 to confirm interface compatibility between two vendors' network LRUs.
practice.question: Which organization publishes the applicable standard?
choices: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization publishes it.
The International Civil Aviation Organization publishes it.
The Federal Aviation Administration publishes it.
The Airlines Electronic Engineering Committee publishes it.

preassessment.question: Which organization publishes avionics interoperability standards?
choices: The International Civil Aviation Organization publishes them.
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization publishes them.
The Federal Aviation Administration publishes them.
The Airlines Electronic Engineering Committee publishes them.

questions.learningObjective: Identify an aviation-specific broadband satellite network.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of satellite and navigation networks, identify which provides broadband connectivity services designed for aviation.

flashcard.front: Which satellite network provides broadband connectivity designed for aviation?
flashcard.back: The Inmarsat satellite network, whose aeronautical services include SwiftBroadband and Jet ConneX.

jeopardy.clue: This L-band and Ka-band satellite operator runs aviation services like SwiftBroadband and Jet ConneX.
choices: Inmarsat satellite network
Global Positioning System
VHF omnidirectional range
ADS-B surveillance network

multipleChoice.question: Of the satellite constellations available to corporate operators, which one is purpose-built as an aeronautical broadband provider?
choices: The Inmarsat satellite network provides aviation broadband.
The Global Positioning System provides aviation broadband.
The VHF omnidirectional range system provides aviation broadband.
The ADS-B surveillance network provides aviation broadband.

practice.scenario: A fleet operator wants broadband in the cabin over the North Atlantic and asks which satellite network is specifically designed for that aviation use case.
practice.question: Which network should you name?
choices: The Inmarsat satellite network is aviation-specific broadband.
The Global Positioning System is aviation-specific broadband.
The VHF omnidirectional range system is aviation-specific broadband.
The ADS-B surveillance network is aviation-specific broadband.

preassessment.question: Which satellite network delivers aviation-specific broadband?
choices: The Inmarsat network delivers aviation broadband.
The Global Positioning System delivers aviation broadband.
The VHF omnidirectional range delivers aviation broadband.
The ADS-B surveillance network delivers aviation broadband.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the frequency band providing the highest throughput for satellite in-flight connectivity.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of radio frequency bands, identify which provides the highest data throughput for satellite-based in-flight connectivity.

flashcard.front: Which frequency band provides the highest throughput for satellite in-flight connectivity?
flashcard.back: Ka-band (roughly 26.5 to 40 GHz); higher bandwidth yields higher data rates than VHF, HF, or UHF options.

jeopardy.clue: Operating from about 26.5 to 40 GHz, this band leads the pack for in-flight broadband throughput.
choices: UHF band
Ka-band
HF band
VHF band

multipleChoice.question: Of the RF spectrum options used for airborne broadband, which one delivers the greatest throughput on a SatCom link?
choices: VHF band at 30 to 300 MHz delivers the highest throughput.
Ka-band at 26.5 to 40 GHz delivers the highest throughput.
HF band at 3 to 30 MHz delivers the highest throughput.
UHF band at 300 MHz to 3 GHz delivers the highest throughput.

practice.scenario: An owner is comparing two connectivity packages. One advertises Ka-band service and the other a legacy L-band plan. They ask which provides the highest throughput.
practice.question: Which band should you identify?
choices: UHF band at 300 MHz to 3 GHz offers the highest throughput.
Ka-band at 26.5 to 40 GHz offers the highest throughput.
HF band at 3 to 30 MHz offers the highest throughput.
VHF band at 30 to 300 MHz offers the highest throughput.

preassessment.question: Which band provides the highest throughput for SatCom in-flight internet?
choices: UHF band at 300 MHz to 3 GHz provides the highest throughput.
Ka-band at 26.5 to 40 GHz provides the highest throughput.
HF band at 3 to 30 MHz provides the highest throughput.
VHF band at 30 to 300 MHz provides the highest throughput.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the connectivity system that uses ground-based towers over land.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of connectivity systems, identify which uses ground-based towers to provide internet service to aircraft over land.

flashcard.front: Which air-to-ground connectivity system uses ground-based towers to serve aircraft over land?
flashcard.back: The Gogo Air-to-Ground system; a network of cell-like towers provides coverage in the contiguous U.S. and parts of Canada.

jeopardy.clue: A network of cellular-style towers in the continental U.S. makes this air-to-ground service possible.
choices: Inmarsat SwiftBroadband
Iridium Certus satellite link
Gogo Air-to-Ground system
GPS-based data link

multipleChoice.question: For continental flights, which onboard connectivity product relies on a network of terrestrial cell-like sites instead of orbiting satellites?
choices: The Inmarsat SwiftBroadband service uses ground towers.
The Iridium Certus satellite link uses ground towers.
The Gogo Air-to-Ground system uses ground towers.
A GPS-based data link service uses ground towers.

practice.scenario: A Citation CJ3+ operating primarily in the continental U.S. needs lower-cost connectivity that does not require a satellite terminal on the fuselage.
practice.question: Which air-to-ground system should you recommend?
choices: The Inmarsat SwiftBroadband service matches this need.
The Iridium Certus satellite link matches this need.
The Gogo Air-to-Ground system matches this need.
A GPS-based data link service matches this need.

preassessment.question: Which connectivity system uses ground-based towers over land?
choices: The Iridium Certus link uses ground towers.
The Inmarsat SwiftBroadband uses ground towers.
The Gogo Air-to-Ground system uses ground towers.
A GPS data link service uses ground towers.

questions.learningObjective: Identify a primary technical challenge of satellite-based connectivity.
questions.fullObjective: Given several candidate issues, identify which is a primary technical challenge of satellite-based connectivity in business aircraft.

flashcard.front: What is a primary technical challenge of satellite-based connectivity in business aircraft?
flashcard.back: High latency caused by the long satellite signal path; round-trip delay affects interactive applications.

jeopardy.clue: The long trip up to orbit and back gives satellite internet this unavoidable timing penalty.
choices: Interference from onboard weather radar
Incompatibility with cabin data buses
Excessive weight of flight computers
High latency from the signal path

multipleChoice.question: Among the typical performance issues associated with SatCom connectivity on corporate jets, which one most affects interactive applications?
choices: Excessive weight of flight computers is a primary challenge.
Interference from the onboard weather radar is a primary challenge.
Incompatibility with cabin data buses is a primary challenge.
High latency from the satellite signal path is a primary challenge.

practice.scenario: Passengers on a Falcon 2000LXS complain that video calls over the SatCom link feel laggy even when speed tests look acceptable.
practice.question: Which primary technical challenge best explains the complaint?
choices: Excessive weight of the flight computers best explains it.
Interference from the weather radar best explains it.
Cabin data bus incompatibility best explains it.
High latency from the signal path best explains it.

preassessment.question: What is a primary challenge of SatCom connectivity in business aircraft?
choices: Onboard radar interference is a primary challenge.
Cabin bus incompatibility is a primary challenge.
Flight computer weight is a primary challenge.
High latency from the signal path is a primary challenge.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the interface standard that governs SatCom-to-aircraft network integration.
questions.fullObjective: Given interface standard options, identify which ensures cabin SatCom equipment communicates properly with the aircraft network.

flashcard.front: Which interface standard ensures that cabin SatCom equipment integrates with the aircraft network?
flashcard.back: ARINC published interface standards; these define the electrical and data rules for avionics integration.

jeopardy.clue: Follow these AEEC-published interface specifications and your SatCom LRU will shake hands with the aircraft network.
choices: ARINC published interface standards
NATO military communication protocols
FAA operational safety regulations
ICAO flight plan formatting rules

multipleChoice.question: During a SatCom retrofit, correct handshaking between cabin gear and aircraft buses depends on adherence to which family of published interface specs?
choices: ARINC published interface standards define the integration.
NATO military communication protocols define the integration.
FAA operational safety regulations define the integration.
ICAO flight plan formatting rules define the integration.

practice.scenario: You are drafting an STC data package for a new Ka-band SatCom install and must cite the standard governing data-bus integration with the existing avionics.
practice.question: Which interface standard should you cite?
choices: ARINC published interface standards are the correct reference.
NATO military communication protocols are the correct reference.
FAA operational safety regulations are the correct reference.
ICAO flight plan formatting rules are the correct reference.

preassessment.question: Which interface standards govern SatCom-to-network integration on aircraft?
choices: ARINC published interface standards govern the integration.
NATO communication protocols govern the integration.
FAA operational regulations govern the integration.
ICAO flight plan formats govern the integration.
